title: Proto One

{{Quote|Me? I'm Proto One. One of a kind is how I like to put it.|Proto One|<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />}}
'''Proto One''', also known as '''Proto Number 1''', was a rickety [[Proto Droid]] who rebuilt himself using assorted replacement parts after he was discarded by his original organic masters. Although he resembled an odd mixture of different [[Droid/Legends|droid]] types and walked with the assistance of a [[Cane/Legends|cane]], he was proud that he was his own [[Maker/Legends|Maker]] and considered himself one of a kind. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Proto One was more than a [[Time/Legends|century]] old, and he worked as the proprietor of a [[Boonta scrapyard|scrapyard]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]].

When the [[Starrunner-class starship|starship]] ''[[Sand Sloth]]'' crashed into the scrapyard, Proto One was happy to fix it for its owner, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Kea Moll]]. After completing the repairs, he discovered that Moll's own droids, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], had unwittingly befriended [[BL-17]], a treacherous droid who belonged to the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. Proto One helped thwart BL-17's plan to capture Moll and her friends, and when the nefarious criminals of the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] afterward attempted to seize them, the Proto Droid fired a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] at the [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]] and scared them off.

==Biography==
===Junk droid===
{{Quote|I created me! They junked me after a century of loyal mechanical service. So I repaired myself.|Proto One, in 15 BBY|<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />}}
The [[Proto Droid]]<ref name="storybook" /> who in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]<ref name="15BBY">"[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].</ref> was known both as Proto One<ref name="Polyhedron">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}}</ref> and Proto Number 1<ref name="storybook" /> was originally active before the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[115 BBY]].<ref name="115BBY">"[[A Race to the Finish]]" established that Proto One had been active for over a century. The events of that episode take place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]] according to "[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]''.</ref> After a [[Time/Legends|century]] of mechanical service,<ref name="A Race to the Finish" /> he<ref name="SWE" /> was discarded by his organic masters. The broken [[Droid/Legends|droid]] rebuilt himself<ref name="A Race to the Finish" /> with assorted replacement parts.<ref name="Second Edition">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> Proto One eventually became the proprietor of a [[Boonta scrapyard|scrapyard]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]].<ref name="Second Edition" /> At some point before 15 BBY,<ref name="15BBY" /> he met and took a disliking to the [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire|BL-series]] droid [[BL-17]].<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

One [[Standard day/Legends|day]] in 15 BBY,<ref name="15BBY" /> the [[Starrunner-class starship|starship]] ''[[Sand Sloth]]'' crash-landed in the scrapyard. Proto One approached two of its occupants—the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—who had been ejected from their [[Starship/Legends|vehicle]] during the crash. He introduced himself and earned the affection of R2-D2, but C-3PO was reluctant to trust him, even after he directed the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid's]] three [[Human/Legends|Human]] masters to a nearby [[Medcenter/Legends|medical unit]] to tend to the injured [[Limb/Legends|arm]] of [[Jord Dusat]], one of their own. Before the five newcomers departed in their [[C/L-82 landspeeder|landspeeder]], the ''[[White Witch]]'', Proto One was recruited to fix the ''Sand Sloth'' by its owner, [[Kea Moll]].<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

===Showdown with BL-17===
{{Quote|If it isn't BL-17, the lowest form of synthetic life to ever crawl across a planet.|Proto One confronts BL-17|<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />}}
[[File:ProtoOneGetsChucked-ARTTF.png|thumb|left|200px|Proto One brawls with BL-17.]]
Proto One completed the repairs before the day's end and left the scrapyard to seek out Moll and the others. He found C-3PO and the ''White Witch'' at a processing plant with BL-17, who had deceptively befriended the protocol droid in order for his master, the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], to capture Moll and her friends. Proto One charged at his old enemy but was easily tossed aside. However, C-3PO realized BL-17's treachery and defeated him when R2-D2 arrived and helped the protocol droid push their foe under a rain of falling machinery. Moll and her friend [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] showed up soon after, but Fett emerged from the shadows and began to fire [[Laser/Legends|laser]] blasts at them. During the commotion, a downed Proto One witnessed [[Tig Fromm]] and [[Vlix Oncard]]—[[Annoo-dat Blue]] [[Gangster/Legends|gangsters]] of the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] criminal organization who had hired Fett and who sought to thwart Moll and her friends—plant a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] aboard the ''White Witch'' and reveal that it would activate after the [[Landspeeder/Legends|vehicle]] completed ten laps of the upcoming [[Boonta Speeder Race]]. By the time Proto One got up and warned Moll and C-3PO, Joben and R2-D2 had already boarded the speeder and fled from a pursuing Fett.<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

Although Joben entered the race before his friends could contact him, he won the event after Fett's own ''[[Silver Speeder]]'' shook the detonator loose and took the brunt of the ensuing explosion. Proto One made his way to the racetrack and saw Oncard, Tig Fromm, and Tig's father [[Sise Fromm]] confront Joben, Moll, Dusat, C-3PO, and R2-D2. The gangsters fled when Proto One fired a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] at them, but a furious Fett captured them and decided to deliver them to their rival [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. Proto One declined an invitation from an apologetic C-3PO to leave Boonta aboard the ''Sand Sloth'', and he waved goodbye to his new friends as they returned to their ship and departed the planet.<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I may be old, but I could run circles around your circuits any day of the millennium!|Proto One, to C-3PO|<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />}}
[[File:RespectYourEldersSon-ARttF.png|thumb|right|200px|Proto One asserts that he is his own Maker.]]
After refitting himself with diverse replacement parts, Proto One resembled a strange mixture of different droid types.<ref name="Second Edition" /> He was in fact a Proto Droid,<ref name="storybook" /> although cobbled-together mechanical beings were more broadly known as junk droids.<ref name="The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide" /> Proto One stood approximately 1.71 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall<ref name="height" /> and was [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] in shape. His head, torso, left hand, and right upper leg were olive-green in [[Color/Legends|color]], while his midsection, [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]], right forearm and left leg were red. His left arm, right upper arm, and right foreleg were gray, as were portions of his face, chest, and his right foot, which was also partly black. Proto One's body sported several scattered red patches, and a red rectangular prism was fitted to his back.<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

The rickety droid<ref name="Polyhedron" /> walked on two legs with the use of a [[Cane/Legends|cane]]; his left foot was roughly the shape of a Human's, but his right foot was conical with three tripodal legs of its own. Proto One had three fingers and an opposable thumb on each hand. His face sported red piping above his photoreceptors that simulated [[Eye/Legends|eyebrows]], and additional red piping around the back of his head gave him the look of a balding Human. The bottom of his face contained his [[Vocabulator/Legends|vocabulator]] and was vertically elongated to resemble a chin and [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]].<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

Proto One was friendly and helpful to strangers who wandered into his scrapyard, but he was quick to beligerently point out that he was his own [[Maker/Legends|Maker]]. Despite the junk droid's mostly polite demeanor, BL-17 earned his ire at some point in his operational history. R2-D2 took an instant shine to Proto One, but C-3PO was hesitant to trust the old droid until he discovered BL-17's deception. A skilled [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], Proto One was confident in his abilities despite his advanced age, and he was happy to fix the ''Sand Sloth'' for Kea Moll and her friends. He considered Boonta to be his home and declined an invitation from C-3PO to leave the planet. Proto One spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and also understood [[Binary/Legends|Binary]].<ref name="A Race to the Finish" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|As to unproduced figures, I'd love to have Proto One, the droid who helps the kids against Boba Fett—or the droids from 'The Great Heap ''[sic]''' episode.|Jeffery Carlisle|<ref name="25 years">{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/droids_ewoks_25th_anniversary/index.html|text=25 Years of Collecting ''Droids'' and ''Ewoks''!}}</ref>}}
Proto One first appeared in "[[A Race to the Finish]]," a [[1985]] episode of the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] cartoon series.<ref name="A Race to the Finish" /> The episode was written by [[Peter Sauder]] and [[Steven Wright]], directed by [[Raymond Jafelice]], and first broadcast on television on [[September 28]], 1985.<ref name="celibration">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> In [[1987]], the episode was adapted into a [[A Race to the Finish (book)|children's storybook]] by [[Dragon Picture Books]], which first identified the character as both "Proto Number 1" and a "Proto Droid." The book does not include Proto One in its climax, instead depicting Boba Fett as seizing the Fromm Gang immediately after they confront the heroes in the wake of the Boonta race.<ref name="storybook" />

A [[Star Wars: Droids (toy line)|''Droids'' toy line]] was produced by [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] toys in 1985, and although it did not include a Proto One figure, ''Star Wars'' artist [[Jeffery Carlisle]] named the character in an interview on [[StarWars.com]] as a toy he wished had been produced.<ref name="25 years" />
